bitty documentation

Documentation

data-* attributes

Overview

  • The HTML side of the bitty API consists of these four attributes.
  • Attributes can be added to HTML elements to send and receive signals through JavaScript methods.

Items

data-connect
Description
  • `data-connect` is an optional attribute that can be added to bitty-8-0 tags.
  • If a `data-connect` attribute exists bitty will attempt to load the module defined by the attribute's value.
  • If a bitty-8-0 tag doesn't have a `data-connect` attribute it will attempt to load a module class from `window.BittyClass` as a fallback.
Examples
Load a default class from `window.BittyClass` if no `data-connect` attribute exists on a `bitty-8-0` tag. [0010-default-bitty-class-on-window]
HTML
<bitty-8-0></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_DA553">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.BittyClass = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_DA553");
  }

  signal_DA553(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Load a `window.NamedClass` directly from that page when the value of `data-connect` is `NamedClass` (and not a remote path). [0020-connect-to-class-on-page]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5EF10"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_5EF10">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5EF10 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_5EF10");
  }

  signal_5EF10(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Connect to the default class from a remote module. [0030-connection-to-default-module-class]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0010-data-attributes/items/data-connect/tests/0030-connection-to-default-module-class/remote-javascript.js"
></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_15881">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
// this content is in an external file
// TODO: show the external file here.
</script>
Results
bug
Connect to a specific class from a remote module. [0040-connect-to-specific-remote-class]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-connect="/versions/8/0/0/documentation/includes/0010-data-attributes/items/data-connect/tests/0040-connect-to-specific-remote-class/remote-javascript.js:ExampleClass"
></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_CD4FD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
// this content is in an external file
// TODO: show the external file here.
</script>
Results
bug
data-listeners
Description
Examples
Listen for a specific event type. [0010-listen-for-event]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9CE5F"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-9CE5F" 
  data-listeners="bittylocallistenertest"
  data-send="signal_9CE5F"
></div>

<div class="test" data-receive="signal_9CE5F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9CE5F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-9CE5F");
    const testEvent = new TestEventClass9CE5F();
    testSender.dispatchEvent(testEvent);
  }

  signal_9CE5F(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};

// This test uses a custom event instead of
// something like mouseover to facilitate
// automated test output.
class TestEventClass9CE5F extends Event {
  constructor() {
    super("bittylocallistenertest", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
</script>
Results
bug
Only send signals when the specified listener is fired (i.e. don't respond to `click` or `input` unless they are included in the `data-listeners` value). [0020-only-send-for-specified-events]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC23DC"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-C23DC" 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousevent"
  data-send="signal_C23DC"
></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_C23DC">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC23DC = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-C23DC").click();
  }

  signal_C23DC(_, el) {
    // set to `bug` here since this
    // signal should not fire.
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
Set listeners globally for a bitty class directly from the bitty-8-0 tag. [0030-set-listeners-globally]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="bittygloballistenertest"
  data-connect="ClassAC896"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-AC896" data-send="signal_AC896"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_AC896">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassAC896 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-AC896");
    const testEvent = new TestEventClassAC896();
    testSender.dispatchEvent(testEvent);
  }

  signal_AC896(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};

// This test uses a custom event instead of
// something like mouseover to facilitate
// automated test output.
class TestEventClassAC896 extends Event {
  constructor() {
    super("bittygloballistenertest", { bubbles: true });
  }
}
</script>
Results
bug
Setting a global `data-listeners` attribute on a `bitty-8-0` tag removes the default `click` and `input` listeners. [0035-global-listener-removes-defaults]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousgloballistenertest"
  data-connect="ClassE7E76"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-E7E76" data-send="signal_E7E76"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_E7E76">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE7E76 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-E7E76").click();
  }

  signal_E7E76(_, el) {
    // set to `bug` here since this
    // signal should not fire.
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
The default `click` and `input` listeners can be added back in to the `data-listeners` attribute on a `bitty-8-0` element. [0037-add-default-to-globals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 
  data-listeners="miscellaneousgloballistenertest click input"
  data-connect="Class22223"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="el-22223" data-send="signal_22223"></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_22223">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class22223 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-22223").click();
  }

  signal_22223(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Setting an empty string for the value of data-listener on an element prevents it from sending signals. [0040-empty-string-turns-off-listeners]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1AE48"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-1AE48" 
  data-listeners=""
  data-send="signal_1AE48"
></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_1AE48">ok</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1AE48 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-1AE48").click();
  }

  signal_1AE48(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "bug";
  }
};
</script>
Results
ok
The default `click` and `input` events can be added back in alongside specified listeners. [0050-include-default-with-custom]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4BCE1"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="el-4BCE1" 
  data-listeners="mouseover bittytest click input"
  data-send="signal_4BCE1"
></div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_4BCE1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4BCE1 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    const testSender = document.querySelector(".el-4BCE1").click();
  }

  signal_4BCE1(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • The specific set of custom listeners is defined based on elements that exist with a `data-listeners` attribute when the module loads. That is, if a new element is added after a bitty module has initialized with a new listener type in a `data-listener` attribute value it won't be picked up.
data-receive
Description
Examples
Receive a single signal. [0010-single-data-receive]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class90DD9"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_90DD9">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class90DD9 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("signal_90DD9");
  }

  signal_90DD9(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Receive multiple signals. [0020-receive-multiple-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCBE64"></bitty-8-0>
<div 
  class="test" 
  data-receive="signal_CBE64 signal_CBE64_2 signal_CBE64_3"
>bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCBE64 = class {
  #updates = [];

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger(
      `signal_CBE64 signal_CBE64_2 signal_CBE64_3`,
    );
  }

  signal_CBE64(_, el) {
    this.#updates.push("bug");
    el.innerHTML = this.#updates[0];
  }

  signal_CBE64_2(_, el) {
    this.#updates.push("bug");
    el.innerHTML = this.#updates[1];
  }

  signal_CBE64_3(_, el) {
    if (this.#updates.length === 2) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Multiple elements receive the same signal. [0030-multiple-receivers]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class327E5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_327E5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class327E5 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger(`signal_327E5`);
  }

  signal_327E5(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
data-send
Description
Examples
Send a single signal from a click event. [0010-basic-send]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassABF58"></bitty-8-0>
<button id="el-ABF58" data-send="signal_ABF58">
  Test Button
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_ABF58">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassABF58 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-ABF58").click();
  }

  signal_ABF58(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Send a single signal that's picked up by multiple receivers. [0020-single-to-multiple]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class16851"></bitty-8-0>
<button id="el-16851" data-send="signal_16851">
  Test Button
</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_16851">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class16851 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-16851").click();
  }

  signal_16851(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Send multiple signals at the same time. [0030-send-multiple-signals]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class148C2"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-148C2" 
  data-send="signal_148C2 signal_148C2_2 signal_148C2_3"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_148C2_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class148C2 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-148C2").click();
  }

  signal_148C2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }

  signal_148C2_2(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }

  signal_148C2_3(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Signals are processed synchronously and in order by default. [0040-sync-default]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class136FB"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-136FB" 
  data-send="signal_136FB signal_136FB_2 signal_136FB_3"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_136FB">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_136FB_2">bug</div>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_136FB_3">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class136FB = class {
  #count = 0;

  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-136FB").click();
  }

  signal_136FB(_, el) {
    this.#count += 1;
    if (this.#count === 1) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  signal_136FB_2(_, el) {
    this.#count += 1;
    if (this.#count === 2) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  signal_136FB_3(_, el) {
    this.#count += 1;
    if (this.#count === 3) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
bug
bug
Async methods can be awaited. [0050-async-await]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7C304"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-7C304" 
  data-send="await:signal_7C304 signal_7C304_2"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="signal_7C304_2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7C304 = class {
  #time1 = performance.now();

  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-7C304").click();
  }

  async signal_7C304(_, el) {
    await this.sleep(50);
  }

  signal_7C304_2(_, el) {
    const time2 = performance.now();
    if ((time2 - this.#time1) > 45) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Signals can be sent without corresponding `data-receive="SIGNAL"` elements. When that happens, the fire only once. [0060-no-receive-required]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0773E"></bitty-8-0>
<button 
  id="el-0773E" 
  data-send="signal_0773E"
>Test Button</button>
<div class="test" data-receive="verify_signal_0773E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0773E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    document.querySelector("#el-0773E").click();
  }

  signal_0773E(_, __) {
    this.trigger("verify_signal_0773E");
  }

  verify_signal_0773E(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug

bit Methods and Properties

Overview

The different types of content are:

  • [x] Element: [x] add - [x] fetch - [x] load - [x] remove - [x] render - [x] save
  • [] Fragment: [x] add - [] fetch - [] load - [] remove - [] render - [] save
  • [x] JSON: [x] add - [x] fetch - [x] load - [x] remove - [x] render - [x] save
  • [] SVG: [] add - [] fetch - [] load - [] remove - [] render - [] save

Each on gets:

  • add

  • fetch

  • load

  • remove

  • render

  • save

Items

addElement(key, content) [DONE]
Description
  • Adds a element to the `this.element[key]` collection
Examples
Add from a string. [0010-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class3CB11"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_3CB11">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class3CB11 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_3CB11");
  }

  given_signal_3CB11(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_3CB11");
  }

  test_signal_3CB11(_, el) {
    const elementString = `<div>ok</div>`;
    this.addElement("el_signal_3CB11", elementString);
    el.innerHTML = this.element["el_signal_3CB11"].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Creating an element returns a result object with an `ok` value of true if the element was added successfully. [0015-ok-true]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class07129"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_07129">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class07129 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_07129");
  }

  given_signal_07129(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_07129");
  }

  test_signal_07129(_, el) {
    const elementString = `<div>ok</div>`;
    const result = this.addElement("el_signal_07129", elementString);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Creating an element returns a result object with an `ok` value of `false` if the element could not be added. [0018-ok-false]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassADA93"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_ADA93">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassADA93 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_ADA93");
  }

  given_signal_ADA93(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_ADA93");
  }

  test_signal_ADA93(_, el) {
    const newFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const result = this.addElement("el_signal_ADA93", newFragment);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use an existing element. [0020-from-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassDCCBD"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_DCCBD">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassDCCBD = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_DCCBD");
  }

  given_signal_DCCBD(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_DCCBD");
  }

  test_signal_DCCBD(_, el) {
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.addElement("el_signal_DCCBD", newEl);
    el.innerHTML = this.element["el_signal_DCCBD"].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Use a document fragment (in which case the first child of the fragment is used. Any other elements are ignored.) [0030-from-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA2A32"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A2A32">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA2A32 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A2A32");
  }

  given_signal_A2A32(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_A2A32");
  }

  test_signal_A2A32(_, el) {
    const newFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    newFragment.appendChild(newEl);
    this.addElement("el_signal_A2A32", newFragment);
    el.innerHTML = this.element["el_signal_A2A32"].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
If there is more than one element in a document fragment only the first one is used. A warning is logged and returned. [0035-ignore-extra-fragment-els]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1D78E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_1D78E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1D78E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_1D78E");
  }

  given_signal_1D78E(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_1D78E");
  }

  test_signal_1D78E(_, el) {
    const newFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    newFragment.appendChild(newEl);
    const newEl2 = document.createElement("div");
    newEl2.innerHTML = "ok";
    newFragment.appendChild(newEl2);
    const result = this.addElement("el_signal_1D78E", newFragment);
    if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = this.element["el_signal_1D78E"].innerHTML;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Elements are added to storage automatically. (NOTE: Data is are currently stored in localStorage. It should considered private to bitty as the internal mechanisms may change.) [0040-store-in-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC2A34"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C2A34">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC2A34 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_C2A34");
  }

  given_signal_C2A34(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.addElement("el_signal_C2A34", newEl);
    this.trigger("test_signal_C2A34");
  }

  test_signal_C2A34(_, el) {
    const storageData =
      JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("el_signal_C2A34")).data;
    const tmp = document.createElement("template");
    tmp.innerHTML = storageData;
    const verifyStorage = tmp.content.firstChild;
    el.innerHTML = verifyStorage.innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Adding an element with the same key as an existing item overwrites the older one. The returned result is a `warn` with a message about the overwrite. The `result.ok` value is still `true`. [0050-new-overwrite-old]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class758B2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_758B2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class758B2 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_758B2");
  }

  given_signal_758B2(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addElement("el_signal_758B2", "<div>bug</div>");
    this.trigger("test_signal_758B2");
  }

  test_signal_758B2(_, el) {
    const result = this.addElement("el_signal_758B2", "<div>ok</div>");
    if (result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = this.element["el_signal_758B2"].innerHTML;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error if no value was passed in with which to make the element. [0060-error-if-no-value]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class8DF02"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_8DF02">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class8DF02 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_8DF02");
  }

  given_signal_8DF02(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_8DF02");
  }

  test_signal_8DF02(_, el) {
    const result = this.addElement("el_signal_8DF02");
    if (result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error result if something other than a String, Element, or Document Fragment was passed to the method. [0070-error-invalid-input]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class24466"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_24466">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class24466 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_24466";

  test_signal_24466(_, el) {
    const result = this.addElement(this.#key, { misc: "not a valid input" });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_24466");
  }

  given_signal_24466(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_24466");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • TODO: Display parsing HTML Footnote
addFragment(key, content) [DONE]
Description
Examples
Add from a string [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class73BA7"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_73BA7">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class73BA7 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_73BA7";

  test_signal_73BA7(_, el) {
    this.addFragment(this.#key, `<div>x</div><div>ok</div>`);
    el.innerHTML = this.fragment[this.#key].children[1].innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_73BA7");
  }

  given_signal_73BA7(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_73BA7");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and provide success result. [0020-ok-true-on-success]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class40096"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_40096">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class40096 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_40096";

  test_signal_40096(_, el) {
    const result = this.addFragment(this.#key, `<div>x</div><div>ok</div>`);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_40096");
  }

  given_signal_40096(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_40096");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error on fail [0030-ok-false-on-failure]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class129C2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_129C2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class129C2 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_129C2";

  test_signal_129C2(_, el) {
    const result = this.addFragment(this.#key, {
      key: "not a string, element, or document fragment",
    });
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_129C2");
  }

  given_signal_129C2(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_129C2");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
From fragment. [0040-from-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassDBF25"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_DBF25">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassDBF25 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_DBF25";

  test_signal_DBF25(fragment, el) {
    this.addFragment(this.#key, fragment);
    el.innerHTML = this.fragment[this.#key].firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_DBF25");
  }

  given_signal_DBF25(_, __) {
    const fragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const element = document.createElement("div");
    element.innerHTML = "ok";
    fragment.appendChild(element);
    this.send(fragment, "test_signal_DBF25");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
From element. (turns the element into a fragment) [0050-from-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class140A5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_140A5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class140A5 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_140A5";

  test_signal_140A5(element, el) {
    this.addFragment(this.#key, element);
    el.innerHTML = this.fragment[this.#key].firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_140A5");
  }

  given_signal_140A5(_, __) {
    const element = document.createElement("div");
    element.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.send(element, "test_signal_140A5");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Store automatically. [0060-store-automatically]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class46488"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_46488">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class46488 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_46488";

  test_signal_46488(element, el) {
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.fragment[this.#key].firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_46488");
  }

  given_signal_46488(_, __) {
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.addFragment(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this.fragment[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_46488");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Warn if overwriting existing key. [0070-warn-on-overwrite]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class45911"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_45911">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class45911 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_45911";

  test_signal_45911(_, el) {
    const result = this.addFragment(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_45911");
  }

  given_signal_45911(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addFragment(this.#key, `<div></div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_45911");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
No value returns error. [0080-no-value-returns-error]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class6EF24"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_6EF24">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class6EF24 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_6EF24";

  test_signal_6EF24(_, el) {
    const result = this.addFragment(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_6EF24");
  }

  given_signal_6EF24(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_6EF24");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
No key and no value returns error. [0090-no-key-no-value-returns-error]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class41274"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_41274">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class41274 = class {
  #key = "fragment_signal_41274";

  test_signal_41274(_, el) {
    const result = this.addFragment();
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_41274");
  }

  given_signal_41274(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_41274");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
addJSON(key, data) [DONE]
Description
  • Adds a new entry with `key` to `this.json[key]`.
Examples
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a string. [0010-add-from-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class59B7E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_59B7E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class59B7E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_59B7E");
  }

  given_signal_59B7E(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_59B7E");
  }

  test_signal_59B7E(_, el) {
    const jsonString = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
    this.addJSON("data_signal_59B7E", jsonString);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_59B7E"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a JSON object. [0020-json-object-add]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA55AA"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A55AA">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA55AA = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A55AA");
  }

  given_signal_A55AA(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_A55AA");
  }

  test_signal_A55AA(_, el) {
    const jsonObject = JSON.parse(`{ "status": "ok" }`);
    this.addJSON("data_signal_A55AA", jsonObject);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_A55AA"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Create a new `this.json[key]` entry from a JavaScript Object. [0022-objects-added]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class51037"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_51037">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class51037 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_51037");
  }

  given_signal_51037(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_51037");
  }

  test_signal_51037(_, el) {
    const jsObject = { status: "ok" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_51037", jsObject);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_51037"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return result `ok` as `true` when a string is added. [0025-strings-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF4BCA"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F4BCA">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF4BCA = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F4BCA");
  }

  given_signal_F4BCA(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_F4BCA");
  }

  test_signal_F4BCA(_, el) {
    const jsonString = `{}`;
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_F4BCA", jsonString);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return result `ok` as `true` when objects are added. [0027-objects-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF9161"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F9161">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF9161 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F9161");
  }

  given_signal_F9161(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_F9161");
  }

  test_signal_F9161(_, el) {
    const jsonObject = JSON.parse(`{}`);
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_F9161", jsonObject);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return result `ok` as `false` if a string is not valid JSON. [0030-returns-error]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA76D8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A76D8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA76D8 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A76D8");
  }

  given_signal_A76D8(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_A76D8");
  }

  test_signal_A76D8(_, el) {
    const invalidJSON = "this is not valid JSON";
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_A76D8", invalidJSON);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
New entries from strings are added to localStorage under a `data` key. [0040-string-to-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassC117E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_C117E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassC117E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_C117E");
  }

  given_signal_C117E(_, __) {
    localStorage.removeItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_C117E");
    const jsonString = `{ "status": "ok" }`;
    this.addJSON("data_signal_C117E", jsonString);
    this.trigger("test_signal_C117E");
  }

  test_signal_C117E(_, el) {
    const verify =
      JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_C117E")).data;
    el.innerHTML = verify.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
New entries from objects are added to localStorage under a `data` key. [0045-object-to-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassA4EF0"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_A4EF0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassA4EF0 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_A4EF0");
  }

  given_signal_A4EF0(_, __) {
    localStorage.removeItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_A4EF0");
    const jsonObject = { status: "ok" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_A4EF0", jsonObject);
    this.trigger("test_signal_A4EF0");
  }

  test_signal_A4EF0(_, el) {
    const verify =
      JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("bittyJSON_data_signal_A4EF0")).data;
    el.innerHTML = verify.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
JavaScript objects are turned into JSON objects which removes any methods they have. [0050-objects-stringified]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassD35C5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_D35C5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassD35C5 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_D35C5");
  }

  given_signal_D35C5(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_D35C5");
  }

  test_signal_D35C5(_, el) {
    const jsObject = { method_D35C5: () => {} };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_D35C5", jsObject);
    const verify = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("data_signal_D35C5")).data;
    if (verify.method_D35C5 === undefined) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Adding a JSON with an existing `key` overwrites the earlier version. [0080-overwrite-existing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class12A9E"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_12A9E">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class12A9E = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_12A9E");
  }

  given_signal_12A9E(_, __) {
    const initialObject = { status: "failed" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_12A9E", initialObject);
    this.trigger("test_signal_12A9E");
  }

  test_signal_12A9E(_, el) {
    const overwritingObject = { status: "ok" };
    this.addJSON("data_signal_12A9E", overwritingObject);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_12A9E"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error if no argument for the JSON was passed in. [0090-error-on-nothing-passed-in]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE60E9"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E60E9">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE60E9 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E60E9");
  }

  given_signal_E60E9(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_E60E9");
  }

  test_signal_E60E9(_, el) {
    const result = this.addJSON("data_signal_E60E9");
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • Items stored in localStorage are moved under a top level `data` key in the object and stringified. (e.g. `{ "example": "alfa" }` becomes the string `{ "data": { "example": "alfa" } }` in localStorage.
  • The related bit methods work with the same top level `data` key in local storage. Those methods being:
    • `.fetchJSON(url [, options = {}])`
    • `.loadJSON(key, fallback)`
    • `.removeJSON(key)`
    • `.saveJSON(key)`
fetchElement(key, url [, options]) [TODO - Add fallback]
Description
  • Fetch HTML from a URL and store it as an element in `this.element[key]`.
Examples
Fetch an element from a URL. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class49058"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_49058">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class49058 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_49058");
  }

  given_signal_49058(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_49058");
  }

  async test_signal_49058(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    await this.fetchElement("data_signal_49058", url);
    el.innerHTML = this.element["data_signal_49058"].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Pass options to a fetch call. [0020-pass-options]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class37F0A"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_37F0A">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class37F0A = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_37F0A");
  }

  given_signal_37F0A(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_37F0A");
  }

  async test_signal_37F0A(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const options = {
      headers: {
        "x-bitty-test": "data_signal_37F0A",
      },
    };
    // await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_37F0A", this.#url, options);
    //
    // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
    // This test is set to always pass as a result.
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

bug
Use a POST call to fetch an Element. [0025-with-post]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class1B0DC"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_1B0DC">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class1B0DC = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_1B0DC");
  }

  given_signal_1B0DC(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_1B0DC");
  }

  async test_signal_1B0DC(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const options = {
      method: "POST",
    };
    // await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_1B0DC", this.#url, options);
    //
    // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
    // This test is set to always pass as a result.
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

bug
`fetchElement()` has a return value object with `.ok === true` when the fetch succeeds. [0030-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class0938C"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_0938C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class0938C = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_0938C");
  }

  given_signal_0938C(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_0938C");
  }

  async test_signal_0938C(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const result = await this.fetchElement("data_signal_0938C", url);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
If the incoming data is a Document Fragment only the first element is used. A warn message is logged and returned as the result. [0032-fragments-truncated]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE0510"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E0510">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE0510 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E0510");
  }

  given_signal_E0510(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_E0510");
  }

  async test_signal_E0510(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-fragment.xml";
    const result = await this.fetchElement("data_signal_E0510", url);
    if (
      result.level === "warn" &&
      this.element["data_signal_E0510"].innerHTML === "first"
    ) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
The raw text from the url is saved into storage if the fetch succeeds. [0035-add-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class92749"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_92749">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class92749 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_92749");
  }

  async given_signal_92749(_, __) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    await this.fetchElement("el_signal_92749", url);
    this.trigger("test_signal_92749");
  }

  async test_signal_92749(_, el) {
    const storage = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("el_signal_92749")).data;
    const tmp = document.createElement("template");
    tmp.innerHTML = storage;
    el.innerHTML = tmp.content.firstChild.innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Return a result with `level === "error"` and `ok === false` if there's an issue with the fetch. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassEFEFA"></bitty-8-0>
<p>NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in 
  the console. The test is run manually to prevent
  the noise from showing up on initial page load.</p>
<button data-send="signal_EFEFA">Test Manually</button>
<div class="manual-test" data-receive="signal_EFEFA">waiting</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassEFEFA = class {
  async signal_EFEFA(_, el) {
    const url = "/url-that-causes-a-404";
    const result = await this.fetchElement("data_signal_EFEFA", url);
    if (result.level === "error" && result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: This test causes error messages to show up in the console. The test is run manually to prevent the noise from showing up on initial page load.

waiting
If a fetch overwrites an existing `this.element[key]` an warning is logged and returned with `level === "warn"` and `ok === true`. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class97CD8"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_97CD8">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class97CD8 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_97CD8");
  }

  given_signal_97CD8(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addElement("el_signal_97CD8", `<div>first</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_97CD8");
  }

  async test_signal_97CD8(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-element.xml";
    const result = await this.fetchElement("el_signal_97CD8", url);
    if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Notes
  • TODO: Display parsing HTML Footnote
fetchFragment
Description
Examples
fetchJSON(key, url [, options]) [TODO - Add fallback]
Description
  • Fetch a JSON from a URL, add it to `this.json[key]`, and store a copy in localStorage
Examples
Fetching a JSON with `this.fetchJSON(key, url)` stores it in `this.json[key]`. [0010-basic-fetch]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class125F4"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_125F4">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class125F4 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_125F4");
  }

  given_signal_125F4(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_125F4");
  }

  async test_signal_125F4(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_125F4", url);
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_125F4"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Pass fetch options with `this.fetchJSON(key, url, options)`. [0020-pass-options]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7878C"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_7878C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class7878C = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_7878C");
  }

  given_signal_7878C(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_7878C");
  }

  async test_signal_7878C(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    const options = {
      headers: {
        "x-bitty-test": "data_signal_7878C",
      },
    };
    // await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_7878C", this.#url, options);
    //
    // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
    // This test is set to always pass as a result.
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

bug
Fetch with a POST method. [0025-with-post]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9D58C"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have
  the ability to modify responses based on 
  request options. Verification is done manually. 
  This automated test is set to always pass
  as a result.
</p>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_9D58C">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9D58C = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_9D58C");
  }

  given_signal_9D58C(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_9D58C");
  }

  async test_signal_9D58C(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    const options = {
      method: "POST",
    };
    // await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_9D58C", this.#url, options);
    //
    // NOTE: Confirming options must be done manually.
    // This test is set to always pass as a result.
    el.innerHTML = "ok";
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: The documentation/test server does not have the ability to modify responses based on request options. Verification is done manually. This automated test is set to always pass as a result.

bug
Log and return `ok` is `true` when the fetch succeeds. [0030-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassF8955"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_F8955">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassF8955 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_F8955");
  }

  given_signal_F8955(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_F8955");
  }

  async test_signal_F8955(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_F8955", url);
    if (result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Fetched items are added to localStorage. [0035-add-local-storage]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassCEAF0"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_CEAF0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassCEAF0 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_CEAF0");
  }

  async given_signal_CEAF0(_, __) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_CEAF0", url);
    this.trigger("test_signal_CEAF0");
  }

  test_signal_CEAF0(_, el) {
    const verify = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem("data_signal_CEAF0")).data;
    el.innerHTML = verify.status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return error with `ok` is `false` when there's a problem with the fetch call. The full response object is dumped to `console.error()`. The primary strings from the response object are added to the `extraInfo` key in value returned from `fetchJSON()`. [0040-fetch-issue]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5862A"></bitty-8-0>
<p>
  NOTE: Fetch errors show up in the console. To avoid
  adding noise to the documentation page this test
  is therefor run manually. 
</p>
<button data-send="test_signal_5862A">Test Manually</button>
<pre class="manual-test" data-receive="test_signal_5862A">waiting</pre>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5862A = class {
  async test_signal_5862A(_, el) {
    const url = "/file-that-does-not-exist.json";
    const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_5862A", url);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = JSON.stringify(result, null, 2);
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results

NOTE: Fetch errors show up in the console. To avoid adding noise to the documentation page this test is therefor run manually. 

waiting
Log and return error with `ok` is `false` when the JSON can't be parsed. [0050-parse-issue]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class4734F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_4734F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class4734F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_4734F");
  }

  given_signal_4734F(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.trigger("test_signal_4734F");
  }

  async test_signal_4734F(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/invalid-json.xjson";
    const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_4734F", url);
    if (result.ok === false) {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return warning with `ok` is `true` when an existing `this.json[key]` is overwritten. [0060-overwrite-warning]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class78263"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_78263">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class78263 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_78263");
  }

  given_signal_78263(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addJSON("data_signal_78263", {});
    this.trigger("test_signal_78263");
  }

  async test_signal_78263(_, el) {
    const url = "/versions/8/0/0/documentation/payloads/valid-json.json";
    const result = await this.fetchJSON("data_signal_78263", url);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
getLogLevel() [DONE]
Description
Examples
Return the log level string. [0010-return-log-level-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE1B2F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E1B2F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE1B2F = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E1B2F");
  }

  given_signal_E1B2F(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_E1B2F");
  }

  async test_signal_E1B2F(_, el) {
    const logLevel = this.getLogLevel();
    if (logLevel === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
json[key] [DONE]
Description
  • A collection of JSON objects stored as values for a given `key`.
  • JSONs can be loaded via `addJSON()`, `fetchJSON()`, `loadJSON()` or from script tags with `type="application/json"` and a `data-key` (see TKTKTK section below for more details).
Examples
A basic `addJSON(key, data)` example. See the individual documentation for more details on all the methods. [0010-add-json-example]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassE70E0"></bitty-8-0>

<script type="application/json" data-key="data_signal_E70E0">
  { "status": "ok" }
</script>

<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_E70E0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassE70E0 = class {
  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_E70E0");
  }

  given_signal_E70E0(_, __) {
    this.trigger("test_signal_E70E0");
  }

  test_signal_E70E0(_, el) {
    el.innerHTML = this.json["data_signal_E70E0"].status;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
loadElement(key [, fallback]) [DONE]
Description
  • Load an element from storage into `this.element[key]`.
  • Use the optional `fallback` if there's no element with the given `key` in storage
Examples
Use `this.loadElement(key)` to load an element from storage into `this.element[key]`. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class23191"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_23191">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class23191 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_23191";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_23191");
  }

  given_signal_23191(_, __) {
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.addElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_23191");
  }

  test_signal_23191(_, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.element[this.#key].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an `ok` result with `true` if the load succeeds. [0012-return-ok]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBB0D2"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_BB0D2">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBB0D2 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_BB0D2";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_BB0D2");
  }

  given_signal_BB0D2(_, __) {
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.addElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_BB0D2");
  }

  test_signal_BB0D2(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadElement(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Overwrites an existing `this.element[key]` if one exists. Logs and returns a warning of it does so (the `ok` value is still `true`). [0015-overwirtes-existing]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassFFDD5"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_FFDD5">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassFFDD5 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_FFDD5";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_FFDD5");
  }

  given_signal_FFDD5(_, __) {
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.addElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    this.trigger("test_signal_FFDD5");
  }

  test_signal_FFDD5(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadElement(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "warn") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Strings can be used as fallbacks. [0020-fallback-string]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassB86EE"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_B86EE">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassB86EE = class {
  #key = "el_signal_B86EE";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_B86EE");
  }

  given_signal_B86EE(_, __) {
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_B86EE");
  }

  test_signal_B86EE(_, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    el.innerHTML = this.element[this.#key].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Element fallbacks are used. [0030-fallback-element]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class31037"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_31037">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class31037 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_31037";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_31037");
  }

  given_signal_31037(_, __) {
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_31037");
  }

  test_signal_31037(_, el) {
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.loadElement(this.#key, newEl);
    el.innerHTML = this.element[this.#key].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Document Fragment fallbacks can be used but everything beyond the `.firstChild` is dropped. A warning is logged and returned (but the `ok` is still `true`). [0031-fallback-fragment]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="ClassBC589"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_BC589">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.ClassBC589 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_BC589";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_BC589");
  }

  given_signal_BC589(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_BC589");
  }

  test_signal_BC589(_, el) {
    const newFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    newFragment.appendChild(newEl);
    const result = this.loadElement(this.#key, newFragment);
    if (result.level === "warn" && result.ok === true) {
      el.innerHTML = this.element[this.#key].innerHTML;
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
String fallbacks are stored. [0033-string-stored-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class9D350"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_9D350">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class9D350 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_9D350";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_9D350");
  }

  given_signal_9D350(_, __) {
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_9D350");
  }

  test_signal_9D350(_, el) {
    this.loadElement(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.element[this.#key].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Element fallbacks are added to storage. [0035-element-stored-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class31502"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_31502">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class31502 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_31502";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_31502");
  }

  given_signal_31502(_, __) {
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_31502");
  }

  test_signal_31502(_, el) {
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    this.loadElement(this.#key, newEl);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.element[this.#key].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Document fragment fallbacks are added to storage. [0037-fragment-stored-fallbacks]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class2B7D0"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_2B7D0">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class2B7D0 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_2B7D0";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_2B7D0");
  }

  given_signal_2B7D0(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeElement(this.#key);
    this.trigger("test_signal_2B7D0");
  }

  test_signal_2B7D0(_, el) {
    const newFragment = document.createDocumentFragment();
    const newEl = document.createElement("div");
    newEl.innerHTML = "ok";
    newFragment.appendChild(newEl);
    this.loadElement(this.#key, newFragment);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.loadElement(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.element[this.#key].innerHTML;
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Log and return an error if there if the `key` doesn't exist in storage and there's no `fallback` [0040-fail-missing-key]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class94D78"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_94D78">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class94D78 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_94D78";

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_94D78");
  }

  given_signal_94D78(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");
    this.removeElement("el_signal_94D78");
    this.trigger("test_signal_94D78");
  }

  test_signal_94D78(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadElement("el_signal_94D78");
    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
loadFragment(key [, fallback])
Description
Examples
Load from storage. [0010-basic-load]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class5D60F"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_5D60F">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class5D60F = class {
  #key = "el_signal_5D60F";

  test_signal_5D60F(_, el) {
    this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    el.innerHTML = this.fragment[this.#key].firstChild.innerHTML;
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_5D60F");
  }

  given_signal_5D60F(_, __) {
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.addFragment(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_5D60F");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Report ok on success. [0015-ok-on-success]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class697D1"></bitty-8-0>
<div class="test" data-receive="test_signal_697D1">bug</div>
JavaScript
<script>
window.Class697D1 = class {
  #key = "el_signal_697D1";

  test_signal_697D1(_, el) {
    const result = this.loadFragment(this.#key);
    if (result.ok === true && result.level === "info") {
      el.innerHTML = "ok";
    }
  }

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup
  /////////////////////////////////////////////////

  bittyReady() {
    this.trigger("given_signal_697D1");
  }

  given_signal_697D1(_, __) {
    this.removeFragment(this.#key);
    this.addFragment(this.#key, `<div>ok</div>`);
    delete this.element[this.#key];
    this.trigger("test_signal_697D1");
  }
};
</script>
Results
bug
Error if key doesn't exist and there's no fallback. [0017-error-if-no-key-or-fallback]
HTML
<bitty-8-0 data-connect="Class7EF03"></bitty-8-0>

JavaScript
<script>

  #key = "el_signal_7EF03";

  test_signal_7EF03(_, el) {

    if (result.ok === false && result.level === "error") {

  /////////////////////////////////////////////////
  // Test Setup

    this.trigger("given_signal_7EF03");
  }

  given_signal_7EF03(_, __) {
    this.setLogLevel("none");

};
